title: Harden SSH Configuration
description: Disable root login, password authentication, and set key-only auth. Change default port and limit users.

implementation.linux: Edit /etc/ssh/sshd_config: PermitRootLogin no; PasswordAuthentication no; PubkeyAuthentication yes; AllowUsers admin deploy; Port 22022; systemctl restart sshd

verification: ssh root@host fails; key-only login works; port 22 refused

title: Install and Configure fail2ban
description: Automatically ban IPs with excessive failed login attempts on SSH, web, and mail services.

implementation.linux: apt install fail2ban; configure /etc/fail2ban/jail.local: backend=systemd; bantime=3600; maxretry=3; enable sshd, apache, nginx jails

verification: 3 failed SSH login attempts result in banned IP (iptables/ufw shows drop)

title: Enable SELinux or AppArmor
description: Mandatory access control confines process capabilities. Enforce targeted policy or AppArmor profiles for web server.

implementation.linux: SELinux: setenforce 1; sestatus shows enforcing. AppArmor: aa-enforce /etc/apparmor.d/usr.sbin.nginx; aa-enforce /etc/apparmor.d/usr.sbin.apache2

verification: Web server process cannot read /etc/shadow or write outside web root

title: Harden Kernel Parameters via sysctl
description: Disable IP spoofing, enable SYN cookies, disable ICMP redirects, and harden TCP stack.

implementation.linux: Edit /etc/sysctl.conf: net.ipv4.tcp_syncookies=1; net.ipv4.ip_forward=0; net.ipv4.conf.all.send_redirects=0; net.ipv4.conf.all.accept_redirects=0; kernel.randomize_va_space=2; sysctl -p

verification: sysctl -a shows tcp_syncookies=1; kernel.randomize_va_space=2 enabled

title: Configure iptables/ufw Firewall
description: Default deny policy. Only allow necessary ports: 80, 443, 22022. Enable connection tracking and rate limiting.

implementation.linux: ufw default deny incoming; ufw allow 80; ufw allow 443; ufw allow 22022; ufw enable. Or iptables: iptables -P INPUT DROP; iptables -A INPUT -p tcp --dport 80 -j ACCEPT

verification: nmap scan shows only 80, 443, 22022 open; all other ports filtered

title: Enable Automatic Security Updates
description: Configure unattended-upgrades (Debian) or yum-cron (RHEL) to install security patches automatically.

implementation.linux: apt install unattended-upgrades; dpkg-reconfigure -plow unattended-upgrades. Or yum install yum-cron; systemctl enable yum-cron

verification: Check /var/log/apt/history.log for automatic security upgrades; yum history for RHEL

title: Enable Auditd for System Call Auditing
description: Monitor sensitive files and system calls for unauthorized access or privilege escalation.

implementation.linux: apt install auditd; configure /etc/audit/rules.d/audit.rules; auditctl -w /etc/passwd -p wa -k passwd_changes; systemctl enable auditd

verification: ausearch -k passwd_changes shows recent password file modifications

title: Configure Centralized Logging
description: Forward logs to centralized SIEM or syslog server to prevent tampering and enable correlation.

implementation.linux: rsyslog config: *.* @logserver.example.com:514; enable TLS if available

verification: Logs visible on central log server; local tampering detectable by discrepancy

title: Account Lockout and Password Policy
description: Enforce password expiration, complexity, and lockout after failed attempts using PAM.

implementation.linux: Edit /etc/security/faillock.conf and /etc/pam.d/common-auth; pam_faillock.so deny=5 unlock_time=900

verification: 5 failed login attempts lock account for 15 minutes

title: Disable Unnecessary Services and Ports
description: Remove or disable unused services, daemons, and cron jobs to reduce attack surface.

implementation.linux: systemctl list-unit-files --type=service | grep enabled; systemctl disable telnet; systemctl disable ftp

verification: netstat -tulpn shows only required services listening; no telnet/ftp

title: Secure Mount Options for Filesystems
description: Mount /tmp, /var/tmp with noexec, nodev, nosuid. /boot as read-only if possible.

implementation.linux: Edit /etc/fstab: /tmp     ext4  defaults,noexec,nosuid,nodev  0 0

verification: mount | grep /tmp shows noexec,nodev,nosuid flags

title: Deploy File Integrity Monitoring (AIDE)
description: AIDE monitors system files for unauthorized changes. Schedule daily checks.

implementation.linux: apt install aide; aideinit; mv /var/lib/aide/aide.db.new /var/lib/aide/aide.db; cron daily aide --check

verification: Test: touch /usr/bin/testfile; aide --check reports new file

title: Restrict sudo and Remove SUID Binaries
description: Limit sudo to specific commands. Audit and remove unnecessary SUID/SGID binaries.

implementation.linux: visudo: user ALL=(ALL) NOPASSWD: /usr/bin/systemctl restart nginx; find / -perm /6000 -type f 2>/dev/null

verification: user cannot sudo commands not listed; SUID count decreased

title: Install and Schedule Malware Scan (ClamAV)
description: Regularly scan web root and uploads for malware signatures using ClamAV.

implementation.linux: apt install clamav clamav-daemon; systemctl enable clamav-freshclam; freshclam; 0 3 * * * clamscan -r /var/www/html > /var/log/clamscan.log

verification: Test with EICAR file; clamscan detects and reports it

title: Synchronize Time with NTP
description: Install NTP or chronyd to ensure accurate timestamps for logs and event correlation.

implementation.linux: apt install chrony; systemctl enable chronyd; chronyc sources shows synced servers

verification: timedatectl shows NTP synchronized: yes

title: Set GRUB Bootloader Password
description: Protect single-user mode and boot parameters by setting a GRUB password.

implementation.linux: grub-mkpasswd-pbkdf2; paste hash into /etc/grub.d/40_custom: set superusers="root" password_pbkdf2 ... ; update-grub

verification: Boot into recovery mode prompts for GRUB password

title: Enable TOTP for SSH (libpam-google-authenticator)
description: Require 2FA TOTP codes in addition to SSH keys for privileged access.

implementation.linux: apt install libpam-google-authenticator; run google-authenticator; add auth required pam_google_authenticator.so to /etc/pam.d/sshd; ChallengeResponseAuthentication yes

verification: SSH login requires verification code after key authentication

title: Encrypted Offsite Backups
description: Encrypt backups before transferring to offsite storage. Use duplicity or restic.

implementation.linux: restic init --repo s3:s3.amazonaws.com/bucket; restic backup /var/www/html --exclude-file=/etc/backup-excludes

verification: Restored backup is accessible only with correct decryption password

title: Harden Docker / Container Runtime
description: Run containers as non-root, drop capabilities, use read-only root FS, and limit resources.

implementation.linux: docker run --user=1000 --read-only --cap-drop=ALL --security-opt=no-new-privileges -m 512m ...

verification: docker inspect shows User: 1000, ReadonlyRootfs: true, CapDrop: ALL

title: Disable Core Dumps to Prevent Credential Exposure
description: Core dumps may contain sensitive memory contents including passwords and private keys.

implementation.linux: ulimit -c 0; echo * hard core 0 >> /etc/security/limits.conf; sysctl -w fs.suid_dumpable=0

verification: ulimit -c shows 0; intentionally crash a test process; no core file created

title: Disable SNMP if Not Needed
description: SNMP can expose system information and be a target for community string attacks.

implementation.linux: systemctl stop snmpd; systemctl disable snmpd; apt remove snmpd

verification: netstat shows no UDP 161; snmpwalk against host times out

title: Regular Permission and SUID Audit
description: Schedule weekly cron to find world-writable files, SUID binaries, and .ssh key permissions.

implementation.linux: 0 0 * * 0 find / -xdev -type f \( -perm -0002 -o -perm -4000 \) -ls > /var/log/perms_audit.log

verification: Audit log contains no unexpected world-writable files in /etc, /usr, or /var

title: Enable ASLR, DEP, and Stack Canaries
description: Ensure kernel and compiler hardening features are active: ASLR, NX bit, and stack canaries.

implementation.linux: kernel.randomize_va_space=2 already set via sysctl; gcc stack-protector enabled by default

verification: sysctl kernel.randomize_va_space returns 2; checksec --file=/usr/bin/nginx shows Full RELRO, Canary, NX

title: Secure Log Rotation and Retention
description: Configure logrotate with secure permissions (640 or 600) and sufficient retention for compliance.

implementation.linux: Edit /etc/logrotate.conf: create 0640 root adm; compress; delaycompress; rotate 52

verification: Log files in /var/log are 640 or 600; at least 52 weeks of compressed logs retained
